channel.title: Business News - Markets reports and financial news from Sky

image.title: Business News - Markets reports and financial news from Sky

channel.description: Sky business news provides up to the minute reports on markets, share prices and the world economy, alongside expert business commentary.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026, Sky UK. All Rights Reserved.

item.title: TG Jones owner Modella weighs tilt at Harvey Nichols
item.description: The prolific retail investor, which last month won approval for a deal to avert the collapse of WHSmith's former high street arm, is weighing a bid for Harvey Nichols, the upmarket department store chain.
item.media:description: Harvey Nichols department store in Knightsbridge, London. PRESS ASSOCIATION PhotoWednesday August 14, 2013. Nick Ansell/PA Wire.

item.title: Under-fire pollster YouGov eyes Levings as next boss
item.description: A former boss of the market research firm Kantar's operations in the Americas has been shortlisted to become the next chief executive of YouGov, the under-fire London-listed polling firm.

item.title: Hosepipe ban brought in for millions more people
item.description: More than two million people will face a hosepipe ban after South East Water said it was extending water restrictions.
item.media:description: A garden hosepipe. File pic: Reuters

item.title: Queues close to an hour at Dover ahead of one of the busiest weekends for drivers
item.description: Queues close to an hour formed at the Port of Dover on Friday night, ahead of what's expected to be one of the busiest weekends in years for drivers.
item.media:description: There have been reports of queues up to 45 minutes long at the port of Dover

item.title: Thames Water lenders plot legal fight with Burnham over nationalisation
item.description: The group of investors engaged in a rescue bid for Thames Water are plotting a multibillion pound legal battle with Andy Burnham's new government if it forcibly nationalises Britain's biggest water company.
item.media:description: Ministers have lined up insolvency practitioners to prepare for the potential collapse of Thames Water, Britain's biggest water utility.

item.title: Delivery trick Tesco wouldn't tell us about - but their drivers did | Money newsletter
item.description: More than 200,000 people have now signed up to our free Money newsletter, which brings the kind of content you enjoy in the award-winning Money blog directly to your inbox every week.
item.media:description: A Tesco delivery van. Pic: iStock

item.title: China backlash as British Steel taken into public ownership
item.description: The Chinese government has criticised British Steel being taken into UK public ownership on "national interest" grounds.
item.media:description: General view of the British Steel works in Scunthorpe. British Steel is set to return to public ownership as Sir Keir Starmer announced new laws he said will give the Government "options" to protect the industry and Scunthorpe workers. New powers could be used to nationalise British Steel, 38 years since the firm was first sold off. Picture date: Tuesday May 12, 2026. PA Photo. Photo credit should read: Joe Giddens/PA Wire

item.title: The 'huge' mistake warring couples are making with pensions
item.description: Couples could be giving away cash to their soon-to-be ex by overlooking pensions in divorce settlements, experts have told the Money blog.
item.media:description: Paperwork and rings on the table between two people, perhaps a negotiating couple. Pic: iStock

item.title: PwC to leapfrog rival KPMG as partner pay surpasses £900,000 again | Mark Kleinman blog
item.media:description: PwC is cutting its Russian firm adrift from its global network. Pic: AP

item.title: Economy flatlining as Reeves nears Number 11 exit
item.description: The UK economy grew by 0.1% in May, according to the final set of official figures ahead of Rachel Reeves's widely expected departure as chancellor.
item.media:description: Chancellor of the Exchequer, Rachel Reeves arrives to deliver a speech at the AI Adoption Summit, in the Smith Centre at the Science Museum,
